verses.text: Joseph cannot hold himself in any longer. He cries out — clear every Egyptian from the room. No outsider stands by while he makes himself known. Then he weeps so loudly that Pharaoh's whole household hears him through the walls.

Joseph cannot hold himself in any longer. He cries out — clear every Egyptian from the room. No outsider stands by while he makes himself known. Then he weeps so loudly that Pharaoh's whole household hears him through the walls.
I am Joseph, he says. Does my father still live? The brothers cannot answer. They are paralyzed at his presence. The dreams they once mocked now stand in front of them in royal robes.
Come near, please. They come near. I am Joseph your brother — whom you sold into Egypt. He names the wound directly, but he calls them brothers in the same breath.

Don't be grieved or angry with yourselves for selling me here. God sent me before you to preserve life. Joseph reframes the worst event of his life on the spot. He has been doing this in private for years.

Two years of famine have passed; five more are coming, with no plowing or harvest. God sent me ahead to keep a remnant of you on earth and to save your lives by great deliverance. The whole family's survival was the secret purpose under the pit.

Two years of famine have passed; five more are coming, with no plowing or harvest. God sent me ahead to keep a remnant of you on earth and to save your lives by great deliverance. The whole family's survival was the secret purpose under the pit.

So it was not you who sent me here, but God. He has made me a father to Pharaoh, lord of his whole house, and ruler over all Egypt. The grace is so thorough it can name the very people who wounded him without bitterness.

Hurry — go up to my father and tell him: God has made your son lord of all Egypt; come down to me, do not delay. You shall live in Goshen, near me — you, your children, your grandchildren, your flocks, everything. I will provide for you there; the famine has five years to run.

Hurry — go up to my father and tell him: God has made your son lord of all Egypt; come down to me, do not delay. You shall live in Goshen, near me — you, your children, your grandchildren, your flocks, everything. I will provide for you there; the famine has five years to run.

Hurry — go up to my father and tell him: God has made your son lord of all Egypt; come down to me, do not delay. You shall live in Goshen, near me — you, your children, your grandchildren, your flocks, everything. I will provide for you there; the famine has five years to run.

Look — your own eyes and Benjamin's see that it is my mouth speaking. Tell my father about all my glory in Egypt, everything you have seen, and bring him down quickly. The dreams of chapter 37 have come true; now the family must come live inside the fulfillment.

Look — your own eyes and Benjamin's see that it is my mouth speaking. Tell my father about all my glory in Egypt, everything you have seen, and bring him down quickly. The dreams of chapter 37 have come true; now the family must come live inside the fulfillment.

Then he falls on Benjamin's neck and weeps; Benjamin weeps on his. He kisses all his brothers and weeps over them. After that, the brothers finally talk with him. Twenty years of silence gives way to actual conversation.

Then he falls on Benjamin's neck and weeps; Benjamin weeps on his. He kisses all his brothers and weeps over them. After that, the brothers finally talk with him. Twenty years of silence gives way to actual conversation.

Word reaches Pharaoh's house and pleases Pharaoh and his servants. He tells Joseph: tell your brothers, take wagons out of Egypt, fetch your father, come to me. I will give you the best of Egypt; you will eat the fat of the land.

Word reaches Pharaoh's house and pleases Pharaoh and his servants. He tells Joseph: tell your brothers, take wagons out of Egypt, fetch your father, come to me. I will give you the best of Egypt; you will eat the fat of the land.

Word reaches Pharaoh's house and pleases Pharaoh and his servants. He tells Joseph: tell your brothers, take wagons out of Egypt, fetch your father, come to me. I will give you the best of Egypt; you will eat the fat of the land.

Pharaoh adds: take wagons for your little ones and wives, bring your father, come. Do not worry about your belongings — the good of all Egypt is yours. The pagan king blesses the patriarch's family with imperial resources.

Pharaoh adds: take wagons for your little ones and wives, bring your father, come. Do not worry about your belongings — the good of all Egypt is yours. The pagan king blesses the patriarch's family with imperial resources.

Israel's sons do as commanded. Joseph gives them wagons and provisions for the road. To each he gives a change of clothes; to Benjamin, five changes and three hundred pieces of silver. To his father he sends ten donkeys loaded with the best of Egypt, ten more with grain and food. Every detail says: he is provided for; he is loved.

Israel's sons do as commanded. Joseph gives them wagons and provisions for the road. To each he gives a change of clothes; to Benjamin, five changes and three hundred pieces of silver. To his father he sends ten donkeys loaded with the best of Egypt, ten more with grain and food. Every detail says: he is provided for; he is loved.

Israel's sons do as commanded. Joseph gives them wagons and provisions for the road. To each he gives a change of clothes; to Benjamin, five changes and three hundred pieces of silver. To his father he sends ten donkeys loaded with the best of Egypt, ten more with grain and food. Every detail says: he is provided for; he is loved.

Joseph sends them off with a parting word: do not quarrel along the way. He knows them. Old guilt and new wealth could fracture them on the road. Pastoral wisdom from a brother who was once their target.

They go up out of Egypt and come back to Jacob in Canaan. Joseph is still alive — he is ruler over all Egypt. Jacob's heart faints; he doesn't believe them. The news is too good for grief to receive at first.

They go up out of Egypt and come back to Jacob in Canaan. Joseph is still alive — he is ruler over all Egypt. Jacob's heart faints; he doesn't believe them. The news is too good for grief to receive at first.

They tell him all that Joseph said, and he sees the wagons Joseph has sent. The spirit of Jacob — the old name returns — revives. Israel speaks: it is enough. Joseph my son is alive. I will go and see him before I die. The wagons preach louder than the words.

They tell him all that Joseph said, and he sees the wagons Joseph has sent. The spirit of Jacob — the old name returns — revives. Israel speaks: it is enough. Joseph my son is alive. I will go and see him before I die. The wagons preach louder than the words.
